summary: The food seasoning/snack compliance landscape has seen significant high-risk changes. New contaminant limits are established by Codex and Japan (including agricultural chemical residues), with strict regulatory limits and prohibitions in Singapore (including specific veterinary drugs), and new action levels for aflatoxins and other chemical contaminants in the US. Additive limits require new verification in Japan, Singapore (including non-permitted additives), and the US (prohibited substances). Labelling requirements are critical: India, Taiwan (specific allergens + sulfites), and the US (9 major allergens, specific declaration rules for gluten-free, cochineal/carmine, sulfites, FD&C Yellow No. 5) mandate new allergen declarations and specific labelling rules. The Middle East has new general labelling, Halal food, GM product, and mandatory shelf-life requirements. Singapore also requires ingredient/additive declaration, and Thailand has new prohibited food lists. Japan has new microbiological standards. Moderate changes include updated verification requirements for additives, contaminants, and microbiological criteria across Codex, EU, GCC, India, Indonesia, and Japan, along with general labelling updates.

changes.notes: Codex establishes maximum levels (MLs) for contaminants in food or feed commodities. These MLs represent the maximum concentration legally permitted to protect human health, based on sound scientific evidence, and are kept as low as possible.

changes.newLimit: Mandatory declaration of allergenic ingredients as per FSSAI regulations
changes.notes: Food products, including seasoning and snack products, containing specified allergenic ingredients must declare them on the label. Verification against the Food Safety and Standards (Labelling and Display) Regulations is required for the complete list of allergens and specific declaration rules.

changes.newLimit: Verification required against 'Specifications and Standards for Food, Food Additives, etc. Under The Food Sanitation Act'
changes.notes: Detailed specifications and standards for food additives are established under the Food Sanitation Act.

changes.newLimit: Verification required against the Positive List System
changes.notes: Japan implements a Positive List System for agricultural chemical residues in food, requiring verification of specific limits.

changes.newLimit: Verification required against 'Specifications and Standards for Food, Food Additives, etc. Under The Food Sanitation Act'
changes.notes: Detailed specifications and standards for contaminants in food are established under the Food Sanitation Act.

changes.newLimit: Verification required against 'Specifications and Standards for Food, Food Additives, etc. Under The Food Sanitation Act'
changes.notes: Detailed specifications and standards for microbiological limits in food are established under the Food Sanitation Act.

changes.parameter: General labelling requirements for prepackaged foodstuffs
changes.notes: This technical regulation specifies the general requirements for labelling of prepackaged foodstuffs. Specific details must be verified in the full standard.

changes.parameter: General requirements for Halal food and declaration
changes.notes: This technical regulation sets general requirements for food products to be considered Halal, impacting ingredients, processing, and labelling for Halal claims. Specific details must be verified in the full standard.

changes.parameter: General requirements for unprocessed genetically modified agricultural products
changes.notes: This technical regulation outlines general requirements for unprocessed genetically modified agricultural products, which may necessitate specific labelling or ingredient restrictions for processed foods containing them. Specific details must be verified in the full standard.

changes.parameter: Mandatory shelf life periods for food products
changes.notes: This technical regulation specifies mandatory shelf life periods for food products. Specific details must be verified in the full standard.

changes.notes: An application can be submitted to SFA to evaluate the use of the additive.

changes.notes: As defined under Regulation 29(3) of the Food Regulations, veterinary drugs include antibiotic and oestrogen.

changes.newLimit: All food businesses must comply with regulatory limits

changes.notes: Applies only to prepacked foods for sale in Singapore.

changes.parameter: Crustacean shellfish, mango, peanuts, sesame seeds, milk and goat milk, eggs, tree nuts, gluten-containing cereals, soybeans, fish
changes.newLimit: Mandatory declaration if present as ingredient or food additive
changes.notes: These allergens require mandatory labeling when present as an ingredient or food additive in prepackaged foods.
Mandatory labeling for sulphite if the residue sulphur dioxide is 10 mg/kg or more in prepackaged foods.

changes.newLimit: Prohibited from production, import, or sale. Verification required to identify specific prohibited foods.
changes.notes: A list of foods prohibited from production, import, or sale exists and must be consulted for compliance.
Prohibited substances are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Part 189).
The nine major food allergens are milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame. This list applies to most packaged food products.
This is a general action level for total aflatoxins in foods.
Specific action level for peanuts and peanut products.
Specific action level for pistachio nuts.

changes.parameter: Various chemical contaminants (tolerances, action levels, guidance levels, etc.)
changes.newLimit: Verification required via Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool and referenced guidance documents/regulations (21 CFR parts 109 and 509)
changes.notes: Users must refer to the tool and linked documents for detailed information on commodity definition and application of contaminant levels.
FDA issued a final rule defining 'gluten-free' for food labeling, including compliance requirements for fermented and hydrolyzed foods (effective 08/12/2020).

changes.parameter: Allergen preventive controls and accurate labeling
changes.notes: Facilities must have written procedures to control allergen cross-contact and ensure accurate allergen labeling, as per the 'Current Good Manufacturing Practice, Hazard Analysis, and Risk-Based Preventive Controls for Human Food' rule (21 CFR part 117).

changes.parameter: Declaration of cochineal extract and carmine
changes.notes: Identified as allergenic substances derived from insects.

The food source of a major food allergen must appear either in parentheses following the ingredient name or immediately after/next to the ingredient list in a 'contains' statement. Specific types of tree nuts, fish species, and Crustacean shellfish must be declared.
Required when present in food at or above 10 ppm total sulfites, due to adverse reactions in sensitive individuals.
Required due to potential hypersensitivity reactions in some individuals.

changes.newLimit: Verification required via GSFA Online Database
changes.notes: The Codex General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets conditions for additive use. Specific provisions for food additives, searchable by food additive, functional class, and food category, are available in the online database. Users are encouraged to consult the Preamble for interpretation.

changes.newLimit: Verification required via Codex Committee on Contaminants in Food (CCCF) and General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995)
changes.notes: The Codex Committee on Contaminants in Food (CCCF) establishes and endorses permitted maximum levels or guideline levels for contaminants and naturally occurring toxicants. Specific limits must be verified by consulting the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and other related Codex texts.
